    Home › Aircraft › Boeing 747 Dreamlifter

    Boeing 747 Dreamlifter

    Boeing · Outsize freighter · In service — 4 built

    The 747 that carries 787s.

    Length71.68 m
    Wingspan64.44 m
    Range7,800 km
    MTOW364 t
    Built4
    First flight2006
    Our numbers

    Derived metrics

    Computed by aircraft.fyi from the core specification — see how we calculate these.

    Range per tonne#38 of 40
    21.4 km/t maximum range ÷ MTOW in tonnes
    Span-to-length ratio#22 of 43
    0.90 wingspan ÷ fuselage length
    Weight per metre#6 of 43
    5.08 t/m MTOW ÷ fuselage length
    Range in hours#22 of 40
    8.9 h maximum range ÷ cruise speed
    Sense of scale

    How big is it, really?

    The Boeing 747 Dreamlifter's wingspan, against things you already know the size of.

    Boeing 747 Dreamlifter64.44 m
    Football pitch (width)68 m
    Blue whale25 m
    New Routemaster bus11.2 m
    You (probably)1.75 m
    #9 of 43 by length → #7 by weight → #28 by speed →
    Specification

    The numbers

    Length71.68 m
    Wingspan64.44 m
    Height21.54 m
    MTOW364,235 kg
    Cargo volume1,840 m³ — 3× a 747-400F
    Range≈7,800 km
    Engines4 × PW4062
    First flight9 September 2006
    Built4 (converted 747-400s)

    Compare it

    Put the Boeing 747 Dreamlifter next to something

    vs Antonov An-124 →vs Airbus Beluga →
    vs Boeing 747 vs Antonov An-225 Compare with anything →
    ← PreviousBoeing 747 Next →Boeing 757

    Last verified: 2026-07-14 · Spot an error? business@luck.fyi

    The story

    What makes it different

    The Dreamlifter exists because of a supply chain. The 787's giant composite fuselage sections are built in Japan and Italy, its wings in Nagoya — and shipping them by sea would take weeks. Boeing's answer was to take four used 747-400s and graft on a cargo hold three times the volume of a 747 freighter's, creating the bulging silhouette that makes every airport it visits stop and stare.

    Its party trick is at the back: the entire tail swings open sideways on a hinge — the longest cargo loader in the world slides complete 787 fuselage barrels straight in. The conversion was engineered with Boeing by Evergreen in Taiwan, and the result is deliberately unglamorous: unpressurised in the main hold, unpainted-looking, and flown hard between Japan, Italy, South Carolina and Everett.

    Only four exist, operated by Atlas Air on Boeing's behalf. During the pandemic they moonlighted hauling millions of face masks, and one earned brief fame in 2021 by landing at the wrong airport in Kansas — on a runway considered too short — and taking off again anyway. At 71.68 metres it is the ninth-longest aircraft ever built, and the fattest-looking thing in the sky.

    Variants

    The Boeing 747 Dreamlifter line

    747-400LCF
    Large Cargo Freighter — the official designation for all four conversions.
    Operators

    Who flies the Boeing 747 Dreamlifter

    5YAtlas AirOperates the entire fleet on Boeing's behalf, feeding the 787 production lines in Everett and South Carolina.4→

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
